messages.subject: [R-gui] unknown color name "systemWindowBody"
messages.body_plain:
This color definition is specific to Windows... Use another way to 
define your color on a Mac.
Best,

Philippe Grosjean

P.S.: I had to accept your message manually: you are not supposed to 
post to the forum without first subscribing to it (with the email 
address you use to send message to the list)

..............................................<?}))><........
  ) ) ) ) )
( ( ( ( (    Prof. Philippe Grosjean
  ) ) ) ) )
( ( ( ( (    Numerical Ecology of Aquatic Systems
  ) ) ) ) )   Mons-Hainaut University, Belgium
( ( ( ( (
..............................................................

Samuel Kemp wrote:
> Hi,
> 
> I am trying to use a tk table, with the following code
> 
> require(tcltk)
> tt <- tktoplevel()
> tclRequire("Tktable")
> tkwm.title(tt,title)
> table1 <-
> tkwidget(tt,"table",rows=nrow,cols=ncol,titlerows=1,titlecols=0,height=height+1,width=width+1,
>                      xscrollcommand=function(...)
> tkset(xscr,...),yscrollcommand=function(...) tkset(yscr,...))
> 
> However, the last line causes the following error message
> 
> unknown color name "systemWindowBody"
> 
> This code works fine in Windows XP, but not on my Mac.
> 
> Does anyone have a solution to this problem? Any help would be gratefully
> appreciated.
> 
> Kind regards,
> 
> Sam
> 
> 	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
> 
> _______________________________________________
> R-SIG-GUI mailing list
> R-SIG-GUI at stat.math.ethz.ch
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-gui
>
messages.body_snippet:
This color definition is specific to Windows... Use another way to 
define your color on a Mac.
Best,

Philippe Grosjean

P.S.: I had to accept your message manually: you are not supposed to 
post to

list. It should contain a color that suits you, isn't it?
Best,

Philippe Grosjean

messages.subject: [R-gui] RKWard 0.4.2
messages.body_plain:
Hi Frank,

On Tuesday 31 July 2007, Frank E Harrell Jr wrote:
> On ubuntu-feisty linux rkward does not work for me after apt-get install
> rkward:
>
> ~ harrelfe:rkward
> /usr/bin/rkward.bin: symbol lookup error: /usr/bin/rkward.bin: undefined
> symbol: R_Visible

sorry for not getting back to you any earlier.

I assume you're using R 2.5.0 or later. Unfortunately, RKWard 0.4.2 will only 
work with versions of R up to 2.4.x. So, in order to use RKWard, you will 
either need to downgrade R, or upgrade RKWard to at least version 0.4.6.

Note that we'll have another incompatibility with the upcoming R 2.6.0. There 
is no released version of RKWard that will work with R 2.6, yet, but we 
expect to produce one around the time that R 2.6 is released, officially. 
(The current development version of RKWard should work with the current alpha 
versions of R, though. See 
http://rkward.sourceforge.net/wiki/index.php?title=RKWard_SVN, if you're 
interested in living on the bleeding edge of development).

Regards
Thomas
-------------- next part --------------

> Note that we'll have another incompatibility with the upcoming R 2.6.0. There 
> is no released version of RKWard that will work with R 2.6, yet, but we 
> expect to produce one around the time that R 2.6 is released, officially. 
> (The current development version of RKWard should work with the current alpha

> http://rkward.sourceforge.net/wiki/index.php?title=RKWard_SVN, if you're

>

> Thomas

Thanks very much for the update Thomas.  I think I'll wait until there 
is an official RKWard release for R 2.6 (hoping also that there will be 
an update for 64-bit machines on debian linux for 2.6).

Frank

-- 
Frank E Harrell Jr   Professor and Chair           School of Medicine
                      Department of Biostatistics   Vanderbilt University

messages.subject: [R-gui] rgnome-0.1.9-alpha released
messages.body_plain:
Dear list subscribers,

its my pleasure to announce the first public alpha of RGnome. [1]

RGnome is a GTK based frontend for GNU R, its features includes
 * rconsole with history
 * an object browser
 * a powerful editor based on gtksourceview
 * a help browser
 * a package manager for loading and unloading packages at runtime

RGnome is currently under heavy development, but we would like to
receive
feedback, so please give it a try.

RGnome is written in Python/pygtk.

best regards,

Gordon Bergling

[1] http://rgnome.boltzmann-konstante.de/

I'm wondering if I'm here in the right list: I'm toying with the idea
building my own gui: It should be possible to enter some values in the
front-end, R does the statistics and returns the result. Is that the
right group? And if it is so, where can I find some ideas how to program
such an interface, as starting point?

Thx,
Thomas

messages.subject: [R-gui] build your own gui
messages.body_plain:
On Thursday 27 September 2007, Michael Lawrence wrote:
> [...]
> > http://www.sciviews.org/_rgui/
>
> That's a good list, but includes lots of R GUIs rather than just tools that
> support GUI construction from R. I recommend using the gWidgets package.
> It's a simplified front-end to the low-level GUI toolkits like tcltk and
> RGtk2.

For a custom, specific purpose GUI, one could create the user interface in a 
webpage. The package Rpad has served me excellently, and the interface was 
quite complicated in the end.
Plus, a customer is quick to open a webpage, especially if R resides on a 
server, rather than installing R and various packages in their own computers.

Hth,
Adrian


-- 
Adrian Dusa
Romanian Social Data Archive
1, Schitu Magureanu Bd
050025 Bucharest sector 5
Romania
Tel./Fax: +40 21 3126618 \
          +40 21 3120210 / int.101
